producer_note: Founded 1984 in Ascoli Piceno by Angela and Ercole Velenosi. 148 ha across Marche (5 DOCGs/15 DOCs) and Abruzzo, producing 2.5M bottles from indigenous varieties like Montepulciano, Pecorino, Verdicchio.
DBR Lafite is a multi-property group; in Chile they own Vina Los Vascos (acquired 1988) in Colchagua Valley, 640 ha planted mainly to Cabernet Sauvignon, Carmenere and Syrah. B Corp certified 2023.
Boiron family domaine in Chateauneuf-du-Pape since 1860, six generations. 32 ha including 25.5 ha red and 1.5 ha white CDP plus Cotes du Rhone. Traditional foudre/demi-muid aging.

Founded early 1980s in Cornas; producer/negociant making wines across northern and southern Rhone AOCs (Cornas, Hermitage, Chateauneuf-du-Pape, Condrieu, Saint-Joseph, Tavel). Organic certified since 2015.
Cooperative in the Robertson Valley (Breede River Valley), Western Cape, known for Chardonnay and Shiraz on limestone soils with Indian Ocean cooling influence.
Sparkling wine project by Slovenian winemaker Igor Tomazic using Rebula and Chardonnay grown in Goriska Brda, then aged 30m underwater in the Adriatic Sea.
Founded 1973 by Joe Phelps in St. Helena, Napa Valley. 525 acres across 11 estate vineyards. Created California's first proprietary Bordeaux blend, Insignia (1974). Owned by LVMH.

Founded 2009 by Yoshi and Kyoko Sato (former Tokyo bankers). 3.2 ha of certified organic/biodynamic vineyards on east-facing slopes in the Cromwell sub-region of Central Otago, producing Pinot Noir, Chardonnay and Chenin Blanc.
Iconic Chateauneuf-du-Pape estate in Courthezon owned by the Perrin family. 110 ha planted to all 13 permitted varieties; organic since the 1960s, biodynamic since 1974.

Grower house in Troissy in the Vallee de la Marne; Orban family vineyards since 1770, house founded late 1950s. 25 ha (HVE level 3); known for Pinot Meunier-driven cuvees. Acquired by Rapeneau family in 1996.
Cooperative founded 1925 in Courthezon; the only cooperative in Chateauneuf-du-Pape. 150 grower families farm 580 ha producing Chateauneuf-du-Pape, Gigondas, Vacqueyras and Cotes du Rhone.
Founded 1858 in Elciego, Rioja Alavesa, by the 6th Marquess of Riscal. Pioneered Bordeaux techniques in Rioja; iconic City of Wine complex with Frank Gehry hotel opened 2006.

Biodynamic Muscadet domaine in Le Landreau, Sevre-et-Maine. Run by Guy Bossard from the early 1970s (organic 1975, biodynamic 1988); now owned by Fred Niger. Soil-specific cuvees (Orthogneiss, Granite).
Five-generation family winery in Oslavia within the Collio DOC; bottled first vintage 1964. Specialists in Ribolla Gialla and orange wines on ponca (clayey marl) soils.

Founded 2001 by Lodovico and Piero Antinori in Bibbona, on the northern edge of Bolgheri. 49 ha planted to Bordeaux varieties (Cabernet Franc, Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, Petit Verdot); Michel Rolland consults.

Negociant-eleveur founded 1859 in Chateauneuf-du-Pape, owned by Maison Jeanjean since 1994. Produces Chateauneuf-du-Pape plus wines across Cotes du Rhone, Gigondas, Vacqueyras, Rasteau, Lirac and Tavel.
Founded 1977 by master distiller Aldo Bottega in Bibano di Godega. Produces Prosecco DOC and Conegliano Valdobbiadene DOCG; signature Bottega Gold Prosecco in gold bottle.
Family winery founded 1950 in Serdiana, southern Sardinia. Eight distinct vineyard sites producing Vermentino, Cannonau, Nuragus and other indigenous varieties using lutte raisonnee farming.

Boissonneau family estate in Saint-Michel-de-Lapujade since 1839. Organic Bordeaux Superieur producer; reds blend Merlot/Cabernet Sauvignon/Malbec/Cabernet Franc, whites from Sauvignon Blanc, Semillon, Muscadelle.
Founded 1991 by Norwegian shipowner Dan Odfjell, first vineyards planted 1994. 275 acres certified organic and biodynamic across Maipo, Lontue and Cauquenes valleys; old-vine Carignan specialist.
Founded 1971 by Arnaldo Caprai; led by son Marco since 1988. Leading producer of Sagrantino di Montefalco DOCG with ~400 acres in Montefalco; pioneered modern Sagrantino research.

Independent grower in Vertus, Cote des Blancs; family making Champagne since 1829, current estate established 1997 by Richard and Romain Colin. 8.5 ha across Cramant, Oiry, Vertus, Bergeres-les-Vertus, Sezanne; Blanc de Blancs focus.
Grower-producer in Crouttes-sur-Marne (western Vallee de la Marne), founded 1979 by Francoise Bedel. 8.4 ha biodynamic since 1998 (Demeter); Pinot Meunier-led, low/zero dosage, extended lees aging.

Founded 1884 by Miguel Escorihuela Gascon in Godoy Cruz; Mendoza's oldest continuously operating winery and the first in Argentina to bottle a varietal Malbec (1940s). Sources from across Mendoza including Uco Valley.

Family winery in Castiglione di Sicilia on the north slope of Mount Etna; founded 1865, eponymous label launched 2012. 65 ha of vines up to 1,000m elevation, focused on Nerello Mascalese and Carricante.
Family house in Urville (Aube/Cote des Bar) since 1808; cellars built by Cistercian monks in 1152. 50+ ha planted 70% Pinot Noir; first carbon-neutral certified Champagne producer.
Historic estate founded 1791 in Paarl by Philippus Wolvaart. One of South Africa's largest and most awarded wineries; renowned for Edelkeur noble late harvest and the annual Nederburg Auction.
Grower-producer in Landreville (Cote des Bar) farming 6 ha certified organic since 2010. Natural-wine focus: spontaneous ferments, no added SO2, very low/zero dosage; notable for Pinot Blanc cuvees.

Estate in Cellino San Marco, Salento (Puglia); revived 1999 by Avignonesi-led group, fully owned by the Falvo brothers since 2008. Certified organic since 2005; Negroamaro, Primitivo, Susumaniello, Verdeca focus.
Family-owned Sicilian producer with six estates across the island: Menfi (since 1994), Vittoria, Noto, Etna (Sciaranuova), and Capo Milazzo. Family roots in Menfi date to 1694.
Cooperative founded 1947 in Vincelles by Henri Blin and 28 growers. 105 ha in the Vallee de la Marne dominated by Pinot Meunier; ~500,000 bottles/year, mostly HVE/VDC, with 10 ha organic.

Founded 1966 by Wolfgang Blass in Nuriootpa, Barossa Valley. Large multi-region brand sourcing from across South Australia (Barossa, McLaren Vale, Eden Valley, Langhorne Creek) for tiered Yellow/Gold/Black/Grey/Platinum labels.
Estate in Gallician (Costieres de Nimes) led by Luc Baudet since 2000. 62 ha mainly Syrah with Grenache, Mourvedre, Roussanne, Viognier; sustainable viticulture; signature 'Rhone Paradox' range.
Natural-wine project of Michael Volker and Melanie Drese in Kitzingen, Franconia, founded 2013. 7 ha of biodynamic vineyards in Repperndorf and Kitzingen; bottled unfiltered with no added SO2.
Founded 1974 by Jerry Lohr; 4,000+ acres of estate vineyards across Paso Robles, Arroyo Seco and Santa Lucia Highlands (Monterey), and St. Helena (Napa). Family-owned, top-25 US producer.
